name: define_success_metrics
description: Define, track, and manage KPIs and success metrics for a BA project. Each metric has a baseline, a target, a direction (higher_is_better / lower_is_better / target_value), a measurement method, and a measurement frequency. Use this when the user wants to establish or review how project success will be measured.

action.description: 'add' a new metric, 'update' an existing metric by metric_id, 'list' all metrics for a project, 'remove' a metric by metric_id.

user_id.description: User ID from session context. Always required.

project.description: Project identifier. Required for add and list.

metric_id.description: UUID of the metric. Required for update and remove.

metric_name.description: Short name for the metric. Required for add.

description.description: What the metric measures and why it matters.

unit.description: Unit of measurement (e.g. %, $, days, NPS points).

baseline.description: Current value before the initiative (starting point).

target.description: Goal value to reach by the end of the initiative.

direction.description: Whether success means going up, down, or hitting an exact value.

measurement_method.description: How the metric is measured (e.g. survey, system report, manual count).

frequency.description: How often the metric is measured.

owner.description: Person or team accountable for this metric.

record.description: Full metric record after the operation (add/update).
